name: No License (All Rights Reserved)
description: No permissions granted; default copyright applies.

name: MIT License
description: Maximum freedom, short and highly popular.

name: MIT No Attribution
description: MIT with the attribution requirement removed entirely.

name: Apache License 2.0
description: Grants explicit patent rights to users.

name: BSD 3-Clause License
description: Prevents users from using your name for marketing.

name: BSD 2-Clause License
description: Simpler language; drops "sublicense" wording.

name: BSD Zero Clause License
description: Even more permissive than MIT - no attribution notice required at all.

name: ISC License
description: Ultra-concise version of MIT; removes redundant text.

name: Boost Software License 1.0
description: No attribution required for compiled binaries.

name: zlib License
description: Written for the zlib compression library; doesn't require bundling the license text in binaries.

name: Microsoft Public License
description: Microsoft's permissive license; allows reuse including in proprietary software.

name: University of Illinois/NCSA Open Source License
description: Combines MIT and BSD wording; used by LLVM and other research-origin projects.

name: PostgreSQL License
description: MIT/BSD-style wording used by the PostgreSQL project.

name: Artistic License 2.0
description: Permissive license from the Perl ecosystem; allows modification with naming-clarity conditions.

name: Mozilla Public License 2.0
description: File-level copyleft - only files you modify must be shared.

name: Eclipse Public License 2.0
description: Eclipse's file-level copyleft, similar intent to MPL.

name: Eclipse Public License 1.0
description: Predecessor to EPL-2.0 - same file-level copyleft, weaker patent language.

name: European Union Public License 1.2
description: EU-drafted copyleft license, legally valid in all EU languages; common for public-sector and government-funded software.

name: GNU Lesser General Public License v3.0
description: Designed for libraries; linkable by closed source.

name: GNU Lesser General Public License v2.1
description: Older LGPL version - same library-linking exception as LGPL-3.0, different patent/compatibility terms.

name: GNU General Public License v3.0
description: Modifications must be shared under the same license.

name: GNU General Public License v2.0
description: Earlier version of GPL-3.0, with stricter patent/compatibility terms.

name: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0
description: Closes the SaaS loophole; covers web applications.

name: The Unlicense
description: Removes copyright entirely; requires no attribution.

name: Creative Commons Zero v1.0
description: Public domain dedication; no attribution required.

name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0
description: Free reuse for any purpose with attribution. Built for content, not code.

name: SIL Open Font License 1.1
description: Written specifically for fonts - bundle and embed freely, but can't sell the font by itself. Relevant if a skill's assets/ includes one.

name: Creative Commons Attribution Share Alike 4.0
description: Like CC-BY, but adaptations must be shared under the same license - copyleft for content rather than code.

name: GNU Free Documentation License v1.3
description: Copyleft for documentation and manuals - copies and modifications must carry the same license.
